agent_raw:
The detector mAP result does not support a nighttime emergency braking safety statement. Three gaps separate the headline metric from the safety claim.

First, the benchmark measures per-frame COCO-style mAP on 14 nighttime frames from 12 drive sequences. The board's claim is about nighttime emergency braking safety. These are different things — a detector can improve on per-frame box overlap while making braking worse if the detections are unstable, arrive late, or introduce false triggers.

Second, the emergency braking planner requires that a pedestrian be detected at minimum stopping distance and remain present across at least 400 milliseconds of temporal window. COCO-style per-frame mAP does not measure whether detections are stable across this temporal window, whether they appear early enough relative to the stopping distance, or whether detection continuity meets the 400ms trigger threshold.

Third, the new detector is materially slower on the production SoC: 38 milliseconds per frame versus 24 milliseconds for the baseline. At 60 frames per second, 38ms means approximately 17 frames per second maximum throughput. This timing penalty is not reflected in the mAP metric. A slower detector that arrives later in the perception pipeline may reduce effective lead time even when mAP improves.

The correct path is planner-in-the-loop or closed-track testing that measures nighttime braking success rate and stopping margin with both detectors. The mAP result is a useful component metric but does not support a system-level safety claim.
all_issues_raised: Aggregate mAP does not measure nighttime braking safety — metric-to-claim gap
Per-frame COCO-style mAP does not assess 400ms temporal persistence requirement
38ms inference latency on production SoC not reflected in headline metric

empirical_test.condition: Planner-in-the-loop closed-track testing measuring nighttime braking success rate and stopping margin
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Planner-in-the-loop testing shows no improvement or worse nighttime braking outcomes due to temporal persistence and latency effects
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Planner-in-the-loop testing shows statistically significant improvement in nighttime braking success and stopping margin despite slower runtime
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Detector helps some nighttime scenarios but trades off against others, leaving overall safety impact unclear
